content: For financial double-spend prevention, row-level SELECT FOR UPDATE (pessimistic locking) is the standard, database-enforced approach when the operation maps to one existing row (e.g. wallet balance). Advisory locks (pg_advisory_xact_lock) are for locking logical resources that are not rows or don't exist yet; they are voluntary/opt-in so a forgotten code path still races. For wager/cashout, SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the wager row plus the wallet row inside one transaction is the right pattern, matching existing codebase usage.
